title: Coffee with Cream on Upholstery
stainChemistry: Same triple-component chemistry as coffee-cream-carpet: coffee tannin + milk protein + milk fat. Upholstery adds two constraints: (1) the CODE TAG (W / W-S / S / X) determines whether water-based treatment is allowed, and (2) BATTING / FOAM saturation is the permanent-failure mode — over-wetting carries the stain into the cushion where home chemistry can't reach. Order invariants from the carpet card apply: cold + Dawn → warm (not hot) enzyme → peroxide → vinegar rinse. Adding: damp cloth only, never pour; weighted dry towel after each wet step to prevent batting wick-back.
whyThisWorks: Correct order + cold water + damp-cloth-only + aggressive weighted extraction between rounds = clean without setting protein, darkening tannin, or saturating batting. The most common failure mode on upholstery is someone pouring water or an alkaline cleaner directly on a coffee-cream spill, setting the protein + darkening tannin + soaking batting simultaneously.
defaultAssumption: Living-room couch / favorite chair spill during morning coffee. Fresh + W/W-S code = reasonable home win. S-only code = pro-lane for water-based combination stain. Dried overnight with batting saturation = pro low-moisture extraction, not more home rounds. If cushion cover zips off and is washable, that's often the easiest path.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Good light, white paper towels / cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Find the upholstery code: W / W-S = water-based protocol OK, proceed. S-only = solvent only, coffee + cream is water-soluble chemistry — this is not a DIY match, call a pro. X = vacuum only, pro. Zip-off washable cover = freeze / blot in place, then wash cover cold with enzyme detergent. Blot the spill outside-in with white towels pressed straight down.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cloth, dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen a cloth with cold water. Press on the spot, lift, rotate, press. Transfer coffee + cream into the cloth. Cold only — hot sets milk protein. Never pour — batting saturation = permanent. Weighted dry towel 3–5 min to extract.

spottingProtocol.technique: Lift fresh tannin + fat without setting protein
spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth, soft brush
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in cold water on damp cloth. Work with soft toothbrush. Dwell 5 min. Blot. Dawn is safe on everything — lifts fat + tannin without setting milk protein. No OxiClean yet — alkaline sets protein.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz in WARM (<100°F) water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Enzyme pretreat in WARM water — bath temperature, NOT hot. Damp cloth, press on spot, cover with damp towel, 15–20 min. Digests milk protein without setting it.
W / W-S code + hidden-area color test: 3% peroxide on damp cloth, 5–10 min. Order matters — AFTER enzyme, not before. Don't mix with ammonia / bleach / alkaline.

spottingProtocol.technique: Neutralize + keep tannin on acid side
spottingProtocol.equipment: 1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar rinse on damp cloth 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide, keeps tannin light. Blot dry.
Weighted dry towel 30 min. Swap for fresh, repeat 2–3 rounds. Finish with fresh towels overnight. Fan-dry fully. Extraction matters more than application on upholstery.

steps: 1. Code tag check. S/X = pro. Blot outside-in.
3. Dawn + cold damp cloth + soft brush.
4. Enzyme pretreat WARM (not hot), 15–20 min — BEFORE oxidizer.
5. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth (W/W-S) AFTER enzyme — hidden test first.
7. Weighted dry towels × 2–3 rounds + overnight + fan.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through initial; warm (<100°F) for enzyme; never hot
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets milk protein + spreads tannin
NEVER oxidizer before enzyme — sets protein
NEVER alkaline (OxiClean alone) — sets protein AND darkens tannin
Aggressive extraction on upholstery > aggressive chemistry

homeSolutions.agent: Three Stains in One + Code Check
homeSolutions.instruction: Coffee with cream is THREE stains at once: coffee tannin + milk protein + milk fat. They respond to different chemistries, and some are OPPOSITES. Hot water + milk protein = cooked-in. Alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, ammonia) + protein = set. Alkaline cleaners + tannin = darker brown permanently. So order matters: cold + Dawn, then warm (not hot) enzyme, then peroxide, then vinegar rinse. Also: find your upholstery code (tag under cushion). W / W-S = proceed. S-only = pro, coffee + cream is water-side chemistry, not a match for solvent-only upholstery. X = pro.

Press a thick stack of white paper towels STRAIGHT DOWN on the spill. Lift, move to a dry section, press again. Work outside-in. Do NOT rub — rubbing pushes coffee + cream into the cushion batting where home chemistry can't reach. Keep blotting until the towel stops picking up color.

Dampen a cloth with COLD water, press firmly on the spot. Lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. Transfer liquid out of the upholstery into the cloth. Cold only. Hot = milk protein set permanently. DO NOT pour water on the upholstery — pouring drives the stain into the cushion batting. Weighted dry towel 3–5 min after blotting to pull moisture back up.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Cold Water on a Damp Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 tablespoon Dawn in 2 cups cold water. Dampen a clean cloth, work into the spot with a soft toothbrush for 5 minutes. Blot with a damp cloth. Dawn lifts fresh tannin + fat without setting protein. DO NOT use OxiClean or baking soda at this stage — alkaline sets protein AND darkens tannin.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat in WARM (Not Hot) Water
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve OxiClean Versatile or Biz in WARM water — bath temperature, under 100°F. NOT hot. Warm is necessary for enzyme activity; hot sets the milk protein. Dampen a white cloth, press on the spot, cover with a second damp towel to keep enzyme wet, 15–20 minutes.

If the code is W or W-S: hidden-area test first. Then dab 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5–10 minutes for residual brown. Only AFTER the enzyme step — peroxide before enzyme sets protein permanently.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with ammonia, bleach, OxiClean, or baking soda.

homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 tablespoon white vinegar + 1 cup cool water. Dab on damp cloth, press 5 minutes. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps residual tannin on the light (acid) side. Blot dry.

homeSolutions.agent: Aggressive Dry-Towel Extraction — The Step That Wins
homeSolutions.instruction: Stack heavy dry white towels on the spot, weigh them with books, 30 minutes. Swap for fresh dry towels, repeat 2–3 times. Finish with fresh towels overnight under weight. Fan-dry in the morning. On upholstery, EXTRACTION matters more than application — over-applying chemistry saturates batting and makes things worse, but aggressive extraction pulls contamination up out of the foam.

homeSolutions.agent: Escalate for S-Code, Batting Soak, or Ring Return
homeSolutions.instruction: Call a pro if: the code is S-only (solvent cleaning, not water-based), the cushion batting is obviously saturated, a ring keeps returning after a full cycle, or someone applied OxiClean / baking soda before you got there. Tell them: "Coffee with cream on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated in order: cold + Dawn → enzyme (warm, not hot) → 3% peroxide → vinegar → aggressive weighted extraction. No hot water / no alkaline before enzyme. Need low-moisture extraction, not truck-mount saturation."

steps: 3. Cold damp-cloth blot + weighted extract. Never pour.
4. Dawn + cold water damp cloth + soft brush, 5 min.
6. 3% peroxide damp cloth AFTER enzyme (W/W-S only).
8. Weighted dry towels × 2–3 rounds + overnight + fan.
9. S-only / batting soak / ring return = pro low-moisture extraction.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if the code is S-only / X (pro), if batting is saturated, if ring returns after a full cycle with correct ordering, or if alkaline cleaner was used before the enzyme step.
warnings: NEVER alkaline (OxiClean alone / baking soda) — sets protein + darkens tannin

neverDo: Never OxiClean alone before enzyme step
Never baking soda / ammonia on tannin
Never rub — drives stain into batting
fiberSensitivities: W / W-S upholstery: full protocol OK
S-only: water-side coffee-cream isn't a match — pro

materialWarnings: Three stains in one — order matters
Batting saturation is the upholstery ceiling

escalation.when: S-only or X code, cushion batting saturation, ring returns after a full correctly-ordered home cycle, alkaline cleaner was used before the enzyme step, or cushion cover isn't removable and spill was large.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee with cream on [W / W-S / S / X] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated in order: cold + Dawn → enzyme (warm, not hot) → 3% peroxide → vinegar → aggressive weighted extraction. No hot water / no alkaline before enzyme. [Ring returns / batting soak / alkaline was used before I got here]. Need low-moisture extraction, not truck-mount."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with low-moisture extraction

contextQuestions: Upholstery code — W / W-S / S / X?
Does cushion cover zip off and machine-wash?
Cappuccino / latte (more milk) or splash of cream?
Has OxiClean / baking soda been used already?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Zip-off cushion covers change the card — wash the cover cold with enzyme detergent. On fixed upholstery, batting saturation is the real ceiling. Latte / cappuccino (more milk, more protein + fat) takes more enzyme dwell than splash-of-cream variants.
